zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Crane於1450年之前出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可斯頓哈姆 帕瓦時，她的父親Robert Crane Jr.18歲，母親Anne Ogard7歲。她於大約1470年與Thomas Appleton在英國素福爾可拉文哈姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1504年11月4日在英國素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Crane于1450年之前出生在英国英格兰素福爾可斯頓哈姆 帕瓦时，她的父亲Robert Crane Jr.18岁，母亲Anne Ogard7岁。她于大约1470年与Thomas Appleton在英国素福爾可拉文哈姆结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1504年11月4日在英国素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德，埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Crane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname, most likely for a tall, thin man with long legs, from Middle English cran ‘crane’ (the bird), Old English cran, cron. The term included the heron until the introduction of a separate word for the latter in the 14th century.

variants.name: Craine
meanings: Manx: shortened form of Gaelic Mac (Giolla) Ciaráin ‘son of (the devotee of) Ciarán’, a personal name based on a diminutive of Gaelic ciar, Manx keeir ‘black’. The place Ballacraine on the Isle of Man means ‘Craine's farm’.

variants.name: Corn
meanings: English: nickname from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cran ‘crane’ (see Crane ).
English: metonymic occupational name for a maker or user of hand mills, Old English cweorn.
Americanized form of German, Dutch, Czech, or Jewish   Korn  , or a shortened form of any of the composite names formed with this element.

variants.name: Crank
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): from Middle English cranke ‘lively, lusty, vigorous’, hence a nickname for a cheerful, boisterous, or merry person.
English: possibly in some cases, a nickname from cranuc, a diminutive of Middle English cran ‘crane’ (see Crane ).

Possibly an Americanized form of German Kranke: from Low German Kraneke ‘crane’, applied to someone thought to resemble the bird in some way, or a nickname for a poor physical specimen, from Middle High German kranc ‘sickly, ailing’.

variants.name: Cran
meanings: Dutch: variant of Kraan or one of its variants, such as Craane and Krane .

variants.name: Cornforth
meanings: English (North Yorkshire and Durham): habitational name from Cornforth in County Durham, named with Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ (see Crane ) + ford ‘ford’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Crane was born before 1450, in Stonham Parva, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert Crane Jr., was 18 and her mother, Anne Ogard, was 7. She married Thomas Appleton about 1470, in Lavenham, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 4 November 1504, in Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England, and was buried in Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Crane est née avant 1450, à Stonham Parva, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Crane Jr., avait 18 ans et sa mère, Anne Ogard, avait 7 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Appleton environ 1470, à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 4 novembre 1504, à Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Crane vor 1450 in Stonham Parva, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert Crane Jr. 18 und ihre Mutter Anne Ogard 7. Sie hat um 1470 in Lavenham, Suffolk, England Thomas Appleton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 4. November 1504 in Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England und wurde in Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Crane è nata il prima del 1450 nel Stonham Parva, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Robert Crane Jr., aveva 18 anni e sua madre, Anne Ogard, aveva 7 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Appleton circa 1470, nel Lavenham, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 4 novembre 1504, nel Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England, e fu sepolta a Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Craneが1450年以前にイギリスイギリススフォルクストンハム パーヴァで生まれた時，父Robert Crane Jr.は18歳，母Anne Ogardは7歳であった。約1470年にイギリススフォルクラベンハムでThomas Appletonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1504年11月4日にイギリススフォルクリットル ワルディングフィールドで死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクリットル ワルディングフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Crane(이)가 1450년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 스토함 파르바에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Crane Jr.(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Ogard(은)는 7세였다. 그녀는 대략 1470년 영국 수폴크 레이븐함에서 Thomas Appleton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1504년 11월 4일 영국 수폴크 리틀 월딩필드에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 리틀 월딩필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Crane nasceu antes 1450, em Stonham Parva, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert Crane Jr., tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Anne Ogard, tinha 7 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Appleton aproximadamente 1470, em Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 4 de novembro de 1504, em Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Crane родилась перед 1450 г. в местности стонам парва, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert Crane Jr. было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Ogard, 7. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Appleton около 1470 г. в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 4 ноября 1504 г. в местности литл валдингфиелд, суффолк, Англия и была похоронена в местности литл валдингфиелд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Crane nació antes de 1450, en Stonham Parva, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert Crane Jr., tenía 18 años y su mamá, Anne Ogard, tenía 7 años. Se casó con Thomas Appleton aproximadamente 1470, en Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 4 de noviembre de 1504, en Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
